subject: Ecology
title: Seed Banks in Soil
description: A quick, practical guide to Seed Banks in Soil.

hook.text: Buried in every soil are thousands of dormant seeds waiting for the right signal to sprout.

buildup.text: Some seeds survive decades underground—desert wildflowers can wait 50 years for rain.

discovery.text: Fire, flooding, or soil disturbance triggers germination by exposing seeds to light or heat.

twist.text: After a forest fire, an entire meadow can bloom from seeds that were buried before the trees grew.

climax.text: Soil seed banks are nature's insurance policy against disturbance and climate shifts.

punchline.text: The next forest is already in the ground.

quiz.question: What can trigger buried seeds to germinate?
